data.cheatsheetContent:
Decide whether source law A ({{ equation1 }}) implies target law B ({{ equation2 }}) over all magmas (binary op `*`). Follow this protocol exactly.
  
purpose: A compact single-problem cheatsheet for deciding whether equation1 implies
  equation2 using only information extracted from those two equations.
inputs_available_to_llm:
  A: equation1, the source law
  B: equation2, the target law
decision_policy:
- Follow levels in order and rules by increasing priority.
- If a rule fires, output its verdict and stop.
- Never override a fired rule with free-form algebra.
- If no TRUE rule fires, use the final default FALSE.
normalization:
  bare_source_for_collapse:
  - If A has one side as a single variable and the other side as a product, put the
    single variable on the left.
  - Rename the single variable to x.
  - On the product side, keep full bracketing; rename other variables by first appearance
    to y,z,w,...
  general_source_form:
  - For non-bare exact-form checks, rename variables by first appearance and keep
    full bracketing.
feature_extraction:
  for_each_equation_E:
    size: total variable occurrences on both sides of E
    vars: number of distinct variable letters in E
    imb: sum over variables of abs(left_count - right_count)
    bare: one side of E is a single variable
    LP: leftmost variable on both sides is the same
    RP: rightmost variable on both sides is the same
    SET: left and right sides use the same variable set
    XOR: for every variable, left/right occurrence parity is the same
    AB: for every variable, left/right occurrence count is exactly the same
    uniform_total_counts: all variables have the same total count across both sides
  only_when_A_is_bare:
    rhsVars: number of distinct variables on A product side
    rhsTotals: sorted product-side occurrence counts as a digit string, e.g. 122
    Lx: product-side leftmost variable is the distinguished x
    Rx: product-side rightmost variable is the distinguished x
    xTop: at the top split U*V, x occurs only left, only right, both, or none
    square: product side contains some repeated subterm u*u
    topShape: 'top split shape: v-m, m-v, or m-m'
    x_occurrences: number of occurrences of distinguished x on product side
    bare_normal_form: normalized string x = product_term
  joint_features:
    size_delta: B.size - A.size
    vars_delta: B.vars - A.vars
    imb_delta: B.imb - A.imb
    invariant_drop: A feature is true while corresponding B feature is false
levels:
- level: L0
  name: source_collapse
  instruction: Before general feature extraction, normalize only the source equation
    A when a collapse condition is checked. If a collapse rule matches, evaluate B
    under the indicated collapse semantics and stop.
  rules:
  - id: L0-COLLAPSE-LP-A
    priority: L0.010
    when: A.bare_normal_form == 'x = (x*(y*(z*(x*y))))'
    then:
      verdict: EVALUATE_COLLAPSED_B
      collapse_semantics: LEFT_PROJECTION
    stop: true
    scope: current A when A is a bare source law
  - id: L0-COLLAPSE-LP-B
    priority: L0.020
    when: A.bare_normal_form == 'x = (x*((y*z)*(z*z)))'
    then:
      verdict: EVALUATE_COLLAPSED_B
      collapse_semantics: LEFT_PROJECTION
    stop: true
    scope: current A when A is a bare source law
  - id: L0-COLLAPSE-RP-A
    priority: L0.030
    when: A.bare_normal_form == 'x = ((((y*z)*x)*z)*x)'
    then:
      verdict: EVALUATE_COLLAPSED_B
      collapse_semantics: RIGHT_PROJECTION
    stop: true
    scope: current A when A is a bare source law
  - id: L0-COLLAPSE-CONST-PRODUCT
    priority: L0.040
    when: A normalizes to left side x*y, right side U*V, and every x on the right
      side occurs in V and none occurs in U
    then:
      verdict: EVALUATE_COLLAPSED_B
      collapse_semantics: CONSTANT_PRODUCT
    stop: true
    scope: current non-bare A with product left side
- level: L1
  name: feature_extraction
  instruction: Compute the listed features exactly for the current equation1=A and
    equation2=B. Do not use any corpus-wide frequencies.
  rules: []
- level: L2
  name: separators
  instruction: If A has an invariant that B lacks, answer FALSE immediately.
  rules:
  - id: L2-SEP-LEFT-EDGE
    priority: L2.010
    when: A.LP && !B.LP
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: any current A/B pair
  - id: L2-SEP-RIGHT-EDGE
    priority: L2.020
    when: A.RP && !B.RP
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: any current A/B pair
  - id: L2-SEP-VARSET
    priority: L2.030
    when: A.SET && !B.SET
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: any current A/B pair
- level: L3
  name: false_guards
  instruction: Apply high-confidence FALSE guards that compare only A and B features.
  rules:
  - id: L3-GUARD-BARE-SAME-SIZE-SHRINK
    priority: L3.010
    when: A.bare && !B.bare && A.size == B.size && B.vars < A.vars && B.imb > A.imb
      && A.XOR
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: any current A/B pair
  - id: L3-GUARD-BARE-BINARY-EXPAND
    priority: L3.020
    when: A.bare && A.vars == 2 && !B.bare && B.vars > A.vars && B.size >= A.size
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: any current A/B pair
- level: L4
  name: structural_rules
  instruction: Apply residual structural rules in order after separators and guards
    fail.
  rules:
  - id: L4-TRUE-BARE-WIDE-TO-BINARY
    priority: L4.010
    when: A.bare && A.vars >= 4 && B.vars == 2
    then:
      verdict: 'TRUE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after earlier levels fail
  - id: L4-FALSE-SAME-VAR-COUNT
    priority: L4.050
    when: B.vars == A.vars
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after earlier levels fail
  - id: L4-FALSE-LOWER-IMBALANCE
    priority: L4.060
    when: B.imb < A.imb
    then:
      verdict: 'FALSE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after earlier levels fail
  - id: L4-TRUE-BARE-GROWS
    priority: L4.080
    when: A.bare && A.vars >= 3 && B.size > A.size
    then:
      verdict: 'TRUE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after earlier levels fail
  - id: L4-TRUE-VAR-SHRINK-IMB-GROW
    priority: L4.100
    when: B.vars < A.vars && B.imb > A.imb
    then:
      verdict: 'TRUE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after earlier levels fail
- level: L5
  name: extended_rules
  instruction: Apply only hand-written feature rules that inspect the current A/B
    pair; no exact-source memorization rules are included.
  rules:
  - id: L5-TRUE-UNIFORM-A-TO-LEFT-BARE
    priority: L5.010
    when: A.uniform_total_counts && B.vars < A.vars && B.is_left_anchored_bare_product
    then:
      verdict: 'TRUE'
    stop: true
    scope: only after structural rules fail
final_default:
  id: FINAL-DEFAULT-FALSE
  when: no earlier rule fires
  then:
    verdict: 'FALSE'
  stop: true
required_output_format:
- 'VERDICT: TRUE or FALSE'
- 'REASONING: list extracted A/B features and the first fired rule; if none fired,
  say FINAL-DEFAULT-FALSE'
- 'PROOF: if TRUE, name the rule and why its condition matches; otherwise empty'
- 'COUNTEREXAMPLE: if FALSE, name the separator/guard/default reason; do not invent
  a model unless explicitly available'
input_only_policy:
- Use only equation1 and equation2 from the current problem.
- Derive all features directly from the syntax of those two equations.
- Do not use sample ids, training-set frequencies, validation accuracy, or memorized
  exact problem records.
data.remark: This method reframes a handcrafted “cheatsheet” for deciding equational implication as a systematic, executable pipeline. Instead of relying on informal prompt rules, it first extracts structured features from the source and target equations, then automatically discovers candidate decision rules, validates them using precision and conflict checks, and organizes them into a stratified sequence. The final output is a compact rule program that evaluates rules in order, stops at the first decisive match, and defaults to FALSE otherwise—making the approach reproducible, extensible, and reliable for deployment.
